activities_projects.title: Identifying Democratic Features
activities_projects.objective: Understand the basic features of democracy
steps: Read the section on features of democracy
List the four key features mentioned in the chapter
Write a short explanation for each feature
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify and understand the essential features of democracy
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct listing and explanation of features (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Democracy Definition Activity
steps: Write the word 'Democracy' at the center of the chart
Add meanings such as 'rule by the people'
Include examples of democratic practices
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students explain the meaning of democracy in simple terms
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and creativity of presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Compare Democratic and Non-Democratic Governments
activities_projects.objective: Differentiate between democratic and non-democratic systems
steps: Draw two columns in your notebook
Write features of democratic governments in one column
Write features of non-democratic governments in the other
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the differences between democratic and non-democratic governments

activities_projects.title: Classroom Discussion on Democracy
steps: Discuss the question 'Why is democracy important?'
Allow students to express their views
Write key points from the discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop communication and analytical skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and clarity of ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poster on Democratic Values
steps: Create a poster showing democratic values
Include concepts like equality, freedom, and justice
Display the poster in the classroom
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance of content (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mock Election Activity
steps: Nominate candidates from the class
Conduct a simple voting process
Count the votes and declare the winner
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how elections work in democracy
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and understanding of election process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Rights of Citizens Chart
activities_projects.objective: Learn about citizens’ rights in democracy
steps: List basic rights of citizens
Explain each right briefly
Decorate the chart with symbols of justice and equality
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of citizens’ rights
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness and clarity of chart (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Group Presentation on Democracy
steps: Divide the class into small groups
Assign each group a topic related to democracy
Present the findings to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop teamwork and presentation skills

activities_projects.title: Identify Democratic Practices in Daily Life
steps: Think about decisions taken at home or school
Identify situations where everyone's opinion is considered
Write examples of democratic practices

activities_projects.title: Write a Paragraph on Why Democracy is Important
steps: Think about the advantages of democracy
Write a short paragraph explaining its importance
Include examples from the chapter
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students explain the significance of democracy in society
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of ideas and correctness (10 marks)
